subject: Political Science
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the meaning and importance of power-sharing in democracy
Compare how Belgium and Sri Lanka handled ethnic diversity
Learn the concept of majoritarianism and its consequences
Understand prudential and moral reasons for power-sharing
Identify different forms of power-sharing in modern democracies
Relate power-sharing concepts to real-life democratic systems

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Power-sharing
teaching_strategy: Class discussion on democracy and distribution of power
student_activities: Discuss situations where power should be shared
List examples of power-sharing in school or society

lesson_plan.title: Ethnic Diversity in Belgium
topics_covered: Population and language groups in Belgium
Conflict between Dutch and French communities
teaching_strategy: Use of diagrams to explain government structure
student_activities: Discuss how Belgium solved its conflict

lesson_plan.title: Majoritarianism in Sri Lanka
student_activities: Create a comparison chart between Belgium and Sri Lanka
Discuss the consequences of majoritarian policies

lesson_plan.title: Why Power-sharing is Desirable
topics_covered: Power-sharing as the spirit of democracy
student_activities: Identify examples of prudential and moral reasons

lesson_plan.title: Forms of Power-sharing
student_activities: Draw a chart showing different forms of power-sharing

lesson_plan.title: Application and Revision
student_activities: Discuss real-life examples of coalition governments

teacher_tip: Use real-world examples such as coalition governments and federal systems to help students understand how power-sharing works in practice.
